text: Boda-boda (motorcycle taxi) transport is the most common youth enterprise in Uganda, with over 1 million riders nationwide. Startup costs: second-hand motorcycle (UGX 3,000,000-4,500,000 for Bajaj Boxer or TVS), new motorcycle (UGX 4,500,000-7,000,000 for Honda or Bajaj Pulsar), driving permit Class A (UGX 100,000), third-party insurance (UGX 50,000-100,000/year), reflective jacket and helmet (UGX 80,000-150,000), stage registration fee (UGX 50,000-200,000). Total startup: UGX 3,500,000-5,500,000. Many youth start by riding someone else's motorcycle on a daily or weekly payment plan (UGX 10,000-15,000/day to the owner), saving to buy their own.

Daily operations of a boda-boda business: Start early (6:00-7:00 AM) to catch morning commuters heading to work. Peak hours: 7-9 AM, 12-2 PM (lunch), and 5-8 PM (evening). Fuel consumption: 2-3 litres/day (UGX 10,000-16,000 at UGX 5,200-5,500/litre). Typical daily trips: 10-20 short trips or 5-8 longer ones. Route selection matters — position near hospitals, markets, schools, taxi parks for steady customers. Daily revenue: UGX 30,000-50,000 after fuel costs. Monthly revenue: UGX 800,000-1,500,000. Key expenses: fuel (UGX 300,000-450,000/month), oil changes every 2 weeks (UGX 15,000), tyre replacement every 3-4 months (UGX 40,000-60,000 each), brake pads (UGX 20,000 every 2 months), stage fees (UGX 5,000-20,000/month). Net monthly profit for owner-rider: UGX 400,000-900,000.

Key risks in boda-boda transport: 1) Road accidents — leading cause of death for young men in Uganda. Always wear helmet, avoid night riding, never ride drunk. Medical bills from one accident can wipe out months of savings. 2) Theft — motorcycles are prime targets. Never park unattended in dark areas, install GPS tracker (UGX 150,000-300,000), join a stage for collective security. 3) Regulatory changes — Kampala Capital City Authority (KCCA) periodically enforces bans on boda-bodas in the city centre. Stay informed through your stage chairman. 4) Mechanical breakdowns — poor maintenance leads to costly engine overhauls (UGX 300,000-800,000). Follow service schedule strictly. 5) Hire-purchase traps — some dealers charge 100-200% interest on motorcycle loans. Calculate total repayment before signing. 6) Health risks — long hours cause back pain, exposure to dust and exhaust fumes. Take breaks, drink water, wear a face mask.

Growing your boda-boda business: 1) Save to buy a second motorcycle — hire a rider and earn UGX 10,000-15,000/day passive income per bike. 2) Join ride-hailing apps: SafeBoda (most popular in Kampala), Uber (limited coverage), or Bolt. SafeBoda riders report 20-40% higher earnings due to premium pricing and digital payments. Registration requires: smartphone (UGX 300,000-500,000), valid permit, clean record. 3) Specialize in delivery services — partner with restaurants, pharmacies, and online shops for package delivery (UGX 5,000-15,000 per delivery). 4) Fleet expansion — 5 motorcycles earning UGX 10,000/day each = UGX 1,500,000/month passive income. 5) Transition to taxi (14-seater matatu) — a well-managed boda fleet can fund a minibus (UGX 25,000,000-40,000,000). 6) Start a boda-boda spare parts shop — you already know the market. 7) Form a SACCO with fellow riders for Emyooga funding (UGX 30,000,000 seed capital for registered SACCOs).
